name: Google Agent Development Kit (ADK)
description: Open-source, code-first framework for building, evaluating, and deploying AI agents. Supports workflow agents, multi-agent hierarchies, built-in evaluation, and deployment to Vertex AI Agent Engine. Underlies Google's broader agent stack and is Gemini-optimized but model-agnostic.

evidence.value: Built-in evaluation framework for assessing response quality and step-by-step trajectory against test cases

task_completion_accuracy.methodology: Review of built-in evaluation tooling and reported agent benchmark workflows

evidence.value: Rich tool ecosystem: function tools, OpenAPI tools, MCP tools, Google Cloud tools, and third-party library integrations

tool_use_reliability.methodology: Tool integration testing across function, OpenAPI, and MCP tool types

evidence.value: ADK 2.0 GA adds graph-based, dynamic, and collaborative workflows alongside sequential, parallel, and loop workflow agents

multi_step_planning.methodology: Assessment of workflow agent primitives and graph-based orchestration in ADK 2.0

evidence.value: Session, state, and memory services with in-memory, database, and Vertex AI managed backends

memory_persistence.methodology: Memory and session service architecture evaluation

evidence.value: Callbacks and loop agents enable retry patterns; robust recovery logic remains developer responsibility

error_recovery.methodology: Error handling pattern review and community issue analysis

evidence.value: First-class multi-agent hierarchies with delegation, agent-as-tool composition, and A2A protocol support

agent_collaboration.methodology: Multi-agent hierarchy and delegation capability testing

evidence.value: Sandboxed code execution available via Vertex AI code executor; general tool sandboxing is developer responsibility

tool_sandboxing.methodology: Security architecture review of tool execution paths

evidence.value: Tool authentication framework (OAuth2, API keys, service accounts) plus Google Cloud IAM when deployed on Vertex AI

access_control.methodology: Access control and authentication capability assessment

evidence.value: Documented guidance on guardrails, callbacks for input/output screening, and Gemini safety settings; no automatic injection blocking

prompt_injection_defense.methodology: Review of documented safety patterns and guardrail mechanisms

evidence.value: Per-session state isolation with user- and app-scoped state prefixes; self-hosted deployments control data boundaries

data_isolation.methodology: Data architecture and session isolation review

evidence.value: Apache 2.0 license, fully open source with public development across Python, Java, TypeScript, Go, and Kotlin SDKs
Self-hosted deployments give full control over retention; Vertex AI Agent Engine follows Google Cloud data governance

data_retention.methodology: Privacy architecture review across deployment options

evidence.value: Vertex AI deployments inherit Google Cloud GDPR commitments; self-hosted compliance depends on operator configuration

gdpr_compliance.methodology: Compliance capabilities assessment for framework and managed deployments

evidence.value: Prompts and tool data are sent to the configured model provider (Gemini API by default, others via LiteLLM)

third_party_data_sharing.methodology: Data flow analysis of model and tool integrations

evidence.value: Model-agnostic via LiteLLM including Ollama and other local model backends; framework itself runs anywhere

local_deployment_option.methodology: Deployment options assessment including local model support

evidence.value: Comprehensive docs covering agents, tools, workflows, evaluation, deployment, and safety with quickstarts and samples
Built-in dev UI for step-by-step inspection of events, state, and tool calls; trajectory evaluation against expected steps
Event stream exposes agent reasoning steps, function calls, and state deltas for inspection
Apache 2.0 licensed; announced at Cloud Next April 2025, Python v1.0 May 2025, ADK 2.0 GA May 2026
Active development with frequent releases, multi-language SDK expansion, and growing contributor base since April 2025 launch
Roughly bi-weekly cadence continues: v2.4.0 released 2026-07-07; 1.x maintenance line still receiving patches (v1.36.1 on 2026-07-06)

community_activity.methodology: Community engagement and release cadence analysis

evidence.value: pip-installable with code-first Pythonic API; agents definable in under 100 lines with CLI and dev UI included
Managed scaling via Vertex AI Agent Engine plus Cloud Run and GKE deployment paths
Framework is free (Apache 2.0); costs come from model API usage and optional Vertex AI / Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform services
Logging, tracing integrations, and evaluation tooling; Cloud Trace and third-party observability supported on managed deployments
Python v1.0 stable since May 2025; ADK 2.0 GA on 2026-05-19 with production-focused workflow and deployment improvements

production_readiness.methodology: Production readiness and release maturity assessment

customer-support.notes: Strong fit for multi-agent support systems; underlies Google's Customer Engagement Suite agent stack

code-generation.notes: Capable via code execution tools and workflow agents, though not a purpose-built coding agent

research-assistant.notes: Multi-agent hierarchies with Google Search grounding work well for research pipelines

data-analysis.notes: Good with code executors, BigQuery and Google Cloud tool integrations

content-creation.notes: Workflow agents support multi-stage drafting and review pipelines

financial-analysis.notes: Viable on Vertex AI with enterprise controls; compliance hardening is the builder's responsibility

healthcare.notes: Requires significant compliance work; Google Cloud HIPAA-eligible services help when deployed on Vertex AI

best_for: Teams building multi-agent systems on Google Cloud and Gemini
Developers wanting a code-first framework with built-in evaluation
Enterprises needing a path from prototype to managed Agent Engine deployment
Builders who want model-agnostic flexibility with Gemini optimization
strengths: First-class multi-agent hierarchies, delegation, and A2A protocol support
ADK 2.0 adds graph-based, dynamic, and collaborative workflows
Built-in evaluation framework for response quality and trajectory testing
Open source (Apache 2.0) with Python, Java, TypeScript, Go, and Kotlin SDKs
Clean deployment path to Vertex AI Agent Engine, Cloud Run, or GKE
Model-agnostic via LiteLLM while optimized for Gemini
limitations: Best experience is tied to the Google Cloud and Gemini ecosystem
Tool sandboxing and injection defenses are largely developer responsibility
Rapid release cadence has introduced breaking changes between major versions
Managed features (Agent Engine, Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform) add paid cloud dependency
Younger ecosystem than incumbent frameworks like LangChain/LangGraph

metadata.deployment_type: Self-hosted or managed (Vertex AI Agent Engine, Cloud Run, GKE)
metadata.first_release: 2025 (announced Cloud Next 2025-04-09; Python v1.0 May 2025; ADK 2.0 GA 2026-05-19)
metadata.pricing: Free framework (Apache 2.0); paid when using Vertex AI Agent Engine or Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform plus model API costs
